touches.ai_summary: Lucas Rogers submitted an inbound demo request via website form, citing interest in improving forecast accuracy for their sales team. Initial contact from Head of Sales at ClearVentures, an EdTech company with ~937 employees.
next_steps: Rep to send calendar link for qualification call

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent follow-up email with calendar availability and brief overview of how the platform addresses forecast accuracy challenges. Lucas responded positively, confirming interest and scheduling qualification call.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate with Salesforce and our existing tech stack?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, native Salesforce integration plus 50+ other tools via API

next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Initial qualification call with Lucas. Discussed current forecasting process and pain points. Lucas mentioned he recently joined ClearVentures 3 months ago and inherited a manual forecasting process. Team of 29 reps currently using spreadsheets. Evaluating Gong as well.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the search for a forecasting solution now?
questions_asked.answer: Board pressure for predictable revenue, missed last two quarters

questions_asked.question: What makes your approach different from Gong's forecasting?
questions_asked.answer: We focus on deal-level predictive scoring vs conversation intelligence

next_steps: Lucas to share current tech stack
Schedule deeper discovery with RevOps team

touches.ai_summary: Brief follow-up qualification call to review tech stack Lucas shared. Confirmed Salesforce instance, discussed data quality concerns. Lucas expressed some hesitation about timeline given ongoing Gong evaluation.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take?
questions_asked.answer: 2-3 weeks for core setup, 4-6 weeks for full optimization

next_steps: Schedule discovery call with broader team
Send case study on forecast accuracy improvements

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent email introducing discovery call agenda and requesting Lucas include RevOps analyst Camila Bennett. Shared EdTech customer case study showing 34% forecast accuracy improvement. Lucas confirmed Camila's availability.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled with Lucas and Camila

touches.ai_summary: Pre-discovery alignment call with Lucas to understand internal dynamics. Lucas revealed he's championing this but needs buy-in from SVP of Revenue Levi Lim. Budget approved for ~$25-30K. Concerned about getting exec support given Gong's brand recognition.

questions_asked.question: What matters most to Levi when evaluating new tools?
questions_asked.answer: ROI and time-to-value, he's very metrics-driven

questions_asked.question: Can you provide ROI projections we can use internally?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we'll build a custom model based on your current miss rate

next_steps: Main discovery call tomorrow with Camila included

touches.ai_summary: Quick pre-call with Lucas. Camila had to drop from tomorrow's discovery due to competing priority. Lucas asked to reschedule for next week when full team available. Expressed continued interest but wants all stakeholders present.
next_steps: Reschedule discovery for next week with Camila and Sales Manager Miles Lima

touches.ai_summary: Full discovery call with Lucas, Camila, and Miles. Deep dive into current forecasting workflow, pipeline review cadence, and data quality issues in Salesforce. Camila raised concerns about data cleanliness impacting any AI tool's effectiveness. Miles shared frustration with manual pipeline updates.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle deals with incomplete or messy CRM data?
questions_asked.answer: Our model flags data gaps and provides confidence intervals based on data quality

questions_asked.question: Who owns forecast accuracy today, and what metrics do you track?
questions_asked.answer: Lucas owns it, currently tracking variance to commit but no deal-level scoring

questions_asked.question: What's your pricing model - per user or platform fee?
questions_asked.answer: Per-seat for core users, unlimited automation seats for end users like reps

objections: Concerned about needing extensive data cleanup before launch
next_steps: Rep to send technical requirements doc
Schedule technical evaluation session

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up discovery call focused on use cases and workflow integration. Lucas walked through their weekly forecast call process. Team expressed interest in automated deal health scoring. Camila asked detailed questions about data security and compliance given EdTech regulatory requirements.

questions_asked.question: Do you have SOC 2 Type II certification?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliant, can share security docs

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the scoring model for our specific deal stages?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully configurable to your stages and weighted factors

next_steps: Send security documentation to Camila
Schedule technical eval next week

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Lucas and Camila walking through platform capabilities. Demonstrated deal scoring, pipeline analysis, and Salesforce integration. Camila tested data connectivity with their sandbox. Team impressed with UI and insights, asked about training requirements for 29-seat deployment.

questions_asked.question: What level of training is required for sales managers vs reps?
questions_asked.answer: 2-hour session for managers, 30-min overview for reps, plus video library

questions_asked.question: How are you currently measuring the cost of forecast misses?
questions_asked.answer: Not quantified, but Lucas estimates 15-20% variance costs them board credibility

questions_asked.question: How does this compare to Gong's forecasting module?
questions_asked.answer: Gong focuses on conversation signals, we use broader deal activity and historical patterns

next_steps: Schedule product demo for broader team
Lucas to socialize internally with Levi

touches.ai_summary: Technical deep-dive session rescheduled from original time. Lucas had conflict due to board prep meeting. Rescheduled call covered advanced features including scenario modeling and custom reporting. Camila requested sandbox access for internal testing. Discussed implementation timeline if decision made by month-end.

questions_asked.question: Can we get a sandbox environment to test with our actual data?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, 14-day trial sandbox available after demo stage

questions_asked.question: What happens if we want to expand seats mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: Prorated adds anytime, true-up at renewal

next_steps: Full product demo scheduled
Prepare ROI model for Lucas to present to Levi
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week

touches.ai_summary: Full product demo rescheduled due to Levi joining last minute. Conducted comprehensive demo with Lucas, Camila, Miles, and Levi attending for first 15 minutes. Levi asked pointed questions about ROI and compared to Gong. Left early but seemed engaged. Lucas indicated Levi is cautiously supportive but wants to see pricing.

questions_asked.question: What's the payback period based on our miss rate and deal size?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 4-6 months based on reducing variance by 40-50%

questions_asked.question: How do you differentiate from Gong beyond the scoring approach?
questions_asked.answer: We integrate activity data beyond calls, including email, meetings, and CRM changes

objections: Levi noted Gong has stronger brand and is already being evaluated
next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing for 29 seats
Include competitive comparison with Gong

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent detailed proposal email including pricing for 29 seats at $26,200 ACV with unlimited automation tier. Included ROI calculator, implementation timeline, and competitive positioning vs Gong. Lucas responded same day asking about discount options and contract flexibility.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing given we're a fast-growing EdTech company?
questions_asked.answer: Can discuss based on commitment term and use case scope

next_steps: Schedule call to discuss pricing and terms

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email from rep addressing Lucas's pricing questions and offering 10% discount for annual prepay. Outlined contract terms and renewal structure. Lucas forwarded to Levi and requested exec meeting to finalize decision. Indicated Gong quoted slightly higher but included conversation intelligence.
next_steps: Schedule exec alignment meeting with Levi

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment call with Levi and Lucas. Levi asked detailed questions about model accuracy, data requirements, and differentiation from Gong. Expressed concern about adding another tool vs expanding Gong usage. Rep positioned as complementary but Levi wants to see proof of incremental value. Lucas advocated strongly for the deal-scoring capabilities.

questions_asked.question: Why shouldn't we just expand our Gong contract to include forecasting?
questions_asked.answer: Gong focuses on conversation quality, we provide predictive deal-level intelligence from all activity

questions_asked.question: What's the model accuracy on deals similar to our size and cycle?
questions_asked.answer: 85-90% accuracy within 10% of actual close value for mid-market B2B SaaS

objections: Concerned about tool sprawl and overlapping capabilities with Gong
next_steps: Send additional case studies from EdTech customers
Lucas to build internal business case

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent email with three EdTech case studies and detailed comparison chart showing complementary nature with Gong. Emphasized unique deal-scoring and forecasting automation vs Gong's conversation focus. Lucas responded that Levi is coming around but wants to negotiate pricing given budget constraints.
next_steps: Schedule negotiation call to discuss final pricing

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Lucas focused on pricing flexibility. Lucas pushed for 20% discount citing budget approved at $22K, not $26K. Rep offered 12% discount for annual prepay plus quarterly business reviews. Lucas indicated need to stay under $24K to avoid re-approval from Levi. Discussed reducing seat count vs discount.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with fewer seats and add more later?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, but per-seat cost increases below 25 seats due to tier pricing

questions_asked.question: Is the budget constraint firm or is there flexibility with stronger ROI case?
questions_asked.answer: Firm at $24K unless we show sub-3 month payback, which is unrealistic

objections: Budget ceiling at $24K, needs discount or scope reduction
next_steps: Rep to review discount options internally
Schedule pricing resolution call

touches.ai_summary: Pricing resolution call with Lucas. Rep proposed 15% discount bringing ACV to $22,270 for 29 seats, within budget. Lucas appreciated flexibility but needs to confirm with Levi given Gong also came back with revised pricing. Indicated decision timeline slipping to next week pending exec review.

questions_asked.question: What's included in the unlimited automation tier?
questions_asked.answer: Automated deal health alerts, forecast roll-ups, pipeline reports for all users

questions_asked.question: Where does Gong's revised pricing land?
questions_asked.answer: Lucas shared it's now comparable but requires bundling conversation intelligence

next_steps: Lucas to present both options to Levi
Schedule exec meeting to finalize

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Levi and Lucas to finalize vendor selection. Levi focused on implementation risk and timeline. Expressed preference for single-vendor approach with Gong but acknowledged Lucas's strong advocacy for specialized forecasting. Requested references from similar-sized EdTech customers before final decision.

questions_asked.question: What's the risk if implementation takes longer than projected?
questions_asked.answer: We guarantee 30-day core deployment or extend contract term to compensate

questions_asked.question: Can you connect us with an EdTech customer doing forecasting at similar scale?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, can arrange reference call with 850-employee learning platform customer

questions_asked.question: What would make this a clear yes for you?
questions_asked.answer: Levi wants proof of faster time-to-value than Gong's forecasting module

objections: Prefers single-vendor solution to reduce integration complexity
next_steps: Arrange customer reference call
Final decision meeting next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up exec meeting with Levi and Lucas after reference call. Customer reference was positive on implementation speed and forecast accuracy gains. Levi warming to dual-tool approach. Discussed contract terms, implementation kickoff timeline, and success metrics. Levi requested one final comparison of total 3-year TCO vs Gong bundled approach.

questions_asked.question: What's the total 3-year cost including implementation and training?
questions_asked.answer: $67K total (3 years at $22,270 plus one-time $500 implementation)

questions_asked.question: What's your decision timeline - is there a board meeting or budget deadline?
questions_asked.answer: Board update next week, want decision finalized by Friday

next_steps: Rep to send TCO comparison
Final approval call scheduled

touches.ai_summary: Final approval meeting rescheduled due to Levi's conflict. Lucas joined solo initially, then Levi joined 10 minutes late. Reviewed TCO analysis showing 18% lower 3-year cost vs Gong bundle with faster implementation. Levi asked for mutual success plan and quarterly exec reviews as condition of approval. Lucas confirmed legal review can be completed within 5 days. Verbal approval given pending contract redlines.

questions_asked.question: Can we include quarterly executive business reviews in the contract?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, standard for accounts over $20K ACV

next_steps: Send contract with mutual success plan
Lucas to route for legal and procurement
Target signature by end of week
